Quality Education.reason: The course provides tertiary education (4.3) and professional skills in information security law (4.4), enhancing understanding of legal frameworks for privacy, which promotes knowledge for sustainable development and human rights (4.7).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course trains students in professional skills for handling legal cases, which contributes to productive employment and decent work (Target 8.5).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Information privacy protection is fundamental for building reliable and trusted digital infrastructure (9.1), supporting domestic technology development (9.b), and ensuring secure access to ICT (9.c).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Personal data protection laws are crucial for preventing data-driven discrimination, thereby promoting equal opportunity and reducing inequalities of outcome (Target 10.3).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course directly focuses on Personal Data Protection Law, supporting the rule of law (16.3), fostering accountable institutions (16.6) in data handling, and protecting fundamental freedoms related to privacy (16.10).
